messages.content: Please paraphase following words to be more formal: Diffusion models have been unprecedentedly boosting the efficiency of personalized data and ignited people’s passion of creating multi-modal data with artificial intelligence. With the combination of vision language pretrained models (CLIP, BLIP, etc.), entensive research has been concentrated on leveraging these models’ power in generative tasks (text-to-image, etc.) via a finetuning process.

Diffusion models have significantly enhanced the effectiveness of personalized data and sparked a fervor for generating multi-modal data with artificial intelligence. Researchers have dedicated extensive efforts to harnessing the capabilities of vision language pretrained models such as CLIP and BLIP to improve generative tasks like text-to-image, primarily through a finetuning procedure.

Please paraphase following words to be more formal: However, limited data reference and model’s great parameter amount makes such finetuning difficult. First, naive fintuning on a small set of reference dataset is very likely cause overfitting and catastrophic forgetting. Second, since diffusion models requires several iterations in its denoising process, making its training really slow. Last but not least, limited reference data add difficulites to generate satisfiying personalized data. Thus, how to finetuning diffusion models on limited data still requires more research.

Nevertheless, the challenges of limited data reference and the substantial number of parameters in the model pose significant difficulties in the finetuning process. Firstly, the straightforward approach of applying finetuning to a small reference dataset often results in overfitting and catastrophic forgetting. Secondly, the training of diffusion models is prolonged due to the need for multiple iterations in the denoising process. Additionally, the scarcity of reference data further compounds the complexities associated with generating personalized data that meets desired standards. Consequently, additional research is imperative to explore how to effectively perform finetuning on diffusion models with limited data resources.

Please paraphase following words to be more formal: There are several works in how to finetuning diffusion models to generate photorealistic images. Text inversion utilized clip models to train a embedding representation for reference images. in its text-to-image reference stage, it use this embedding to instruct diffusion models to generate image with reference’s styles. dreambooth introduce a prior-preservation loss with a domain-level prompt, such design aims at overfitting problems.

Numerous studies have focused on the refinement of diffusion models for the production of photorealistic images. For instance, certain approaches have explored the use of clip models to establish an embedding representation based on reference images. During the text-to-image referencing phase, this embedding is employed to guide diffusion models in generating images that inherit the style of the reference images. Another method, known as dreambooth, introduces a prior-preservation loss alongside a domain-level prompt. This particular design is intended to address potential issues related to overfitting.

Please paraphase these words to be more formal: However, these methods is still has limitations. First, these methods only use original reference data, which is still hard to generate photorealitstic photos, especially for photos whose content includes people showing up in these reference. Second, directly apply fintuning with full-precision is still time comsuming, after finetuning, the full-precision model’s deployments still remain challenging.

Nonetheless, these methods have certain limitations that need to be acknowledged. Firstly, they solely rely on original reference data, which poses challenges in generating photorealistic photos, particularly when it involves individuals present in the reference images. Secondly, the process of applying finetuning directly with full-precision models remains time-consuming, and deploying the finetuned full-precision models continues to present challenges.

Please paraphase these words to be more formal: we proposed a parameter efficient finetuning (PEFT) framework to deal with challenge mention above. During the training process, we combined text inversion’s embedding training and add UNet’s bias to trainable parameters. Plus, during training process, we sampled relatively high quanlity results and add them to training dataset to add more personalized reference. Last but not least, we apply QLoRA in training process to obtained a low-bit model.

We have introduced the Parameter Efficient Finetuning (PEFT) framework as a solution to address the aforementioned challenges. In this framework, we incorporate the embedding training from text inversion, and augment the trainable parameters by incorporating UNet's bias. Furthermore, to enhance the training process, we include relatively high-quality results in the training dataset to provide additional personalized references. Lastly, we integrate the QLoRA technique during the training process to obtain a low-bit model.

Please paraphase following words: Our light-weight finetuning process can help Intel better deliver personalized diffusion models’ finetuning performance, more likely to raise their interest in Intel’s AI product. Combination with QLoRA enables our finetuning models to is expected to gain acceleration on Intel’s hardware.

The adoption of our streamlined finetuning process holds the potential to enhance Intel's capability in delivering high-performance personalized diffusion models, which is likely to generate increased interest in Intel's AI product offerings. The integration of QLoRA further amplifies the advantages of our finetuning models, as it is anticipated to provide acceleration on Intel's hardware platforms.

Please paraphase following words: As we mentioned before, directly finetuning the entire diffusion model can result in overfitting and catastrophic forgetting. Thus, we combine light-weight text inversion’s token embedding training in our finetuning process, the new token can be freely defined by users, usually user’s own name. Plus, to make generating process more flexible, we set bias terms in the UNet as trainable items.

As previously mentioned, the direct finetuning of the complete diffusion model can give rise to overfitting and catastrophic forgetting. To address this issue, we integrate the lightweight training of text inversion's token embeddings into our finetuning process. Users are given the freedom to define new tokens, typically using their own names, thereby enhancing the customization aspect. Additionally, to introduce greater flexibility in the generation process, we treat the bias terms in the UNet architecture as trainable components.

Please paraphase following words: Also, we use select some satisfactory generateed data and add them to the training dataset as a data augumentation process. To assess generated images’ quanlity, we use ArcFace to judge if the generated face photorealistic enough. An perceptual loss is also added to assess image’s sementic quality.

Furthermore, we employ a selective approach where we choose certain generated data that meet satisfactory criteria and incorporate them into the training dataset as part of a data augmentation process. In order to evaluate the photorealism of the generated images, we utilize ArcFace to assess whether the generated faces exhibit a high degree of realism. Additionally, we introduce a perceptual loss to evaluate the semantic quality of the images.

Please paraphase following words: To better fit the Intel’s hardware and gain acceleration in real deployment, we introduce quantization process in the finetuning process. We load the model with QLoRA’s config and use page optimizer to locate optimizing states on CPUs, thus, we reduce the memory consumption

In order to optimize performance and achieve acceleration on Intel's hardware during real deployment, we incorporate a quantization process into our finetuning procedure. By loading the model with QLoRA's configuration and utilizing page optimizer, we efficiently optimize states on CPUs, thereby reducing memory consumption.
